channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump takes laps on Daytona 500 speedway in The Beast
item.description: “This is your favorite president,” Trump said. “I’m a big fan. I’m a really big fan of you people. How you do this, I don’t know. But I just want you to be safe.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tries to muscle up as U.S. plots mediation to help end Russia's war in Ukraine
item.description: European leaders are taking steps to answer assertive U.S. efforts to end Russia’s war against Ukraine. France on Sunday announced a meeting of continental leaders and Baltic countries called for new steps to strengthen Kyiv’s hand in any future talks.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion about “getting rid of” the nation's disaster relief agency doesn't sound like a bad idea for some residents of western North Carolina who are still navigating the long recovery process from Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rubio, Netanyahu affirm 'common strategy' for Gaza, set sights on Iran in joint statement
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he shares a "common strategy" for Gaza with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: George has his day, and so does Abe. But states honor US presidents in lots of ways
item.description: U.S. states seem to mark Presidents Day with as many different names and honors as there have been presidents.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon's America: What Elon Musk did before DOGE
item.description: Elon Musk may be the face of DOGE, but he made a name for himself in businesses built around technology and space travel.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration fires more than a dozen immigration judges
item.description: More than a dozen immigration judges were dismissed without notice amid a crisis at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor slams Vance's call for Europe to be inclusive of far-right parties
item.description: Olaf Scholz rebuked U.S. Vice President JD Vance for suggesting that far-right parties should not be shut out of government. Germany's other political parties have refused to work with the far-right AfD.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim and Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: AP reporter and photographer barred from Air Force One over 'Gulf of Mexico' terminology dispute
item.description: The Trump administration has blocked The Associated Press from traveling with President Trump on Air Force One and also from covering an Oval Office event. It's part of an ongoing terminology dispute over the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: 7 most likely GOP successors to Trump in 2028
item.description: President Trump raised eyebrows in an interview that aired Monday after he declined to name Vice President Vance as his automatic successor ahead of the 2028 presidential election. “No, but he is very capable,” Trump told fox News’ Bret Baier when asked if he viewed Vance as his 2028 successor.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump officials signal potential changes at NOAA, the weather and climate agency
item.description: Federal workers at the home agency for the National Weather Service are concerned about a potential overhaul by Trump officials focused on cutting government costs.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Borunda, Michael Copley, and Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: Sarah Palin ‘would love to’ join Trump administration
item.description: She pointed to "gatekeepers" within the administration, telling NewsNation: "It gets frustrating for those with a heart to serve.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Kern County judge confirms Greenfield Union School District election
item.description: He said there is not enough evidence that the illegal ballots affected the outcome of the election. Once a ballot is opened there is no way to trace that ballot back to a voter.
item.dc:creator: Connor Dore, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC public advocate questions Mayor Adams’ ability to govern
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City Public Advocate Jumaane Williams says Mayor Eric Adams can no longer govern the city “the way it needs to be governed.” The criticism came Friday after New York Lt. Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Scrivner’s legal woes shattered family political network
item.description: The saga surrounding Zack Scrivner isn't just a story about his alleged crimes. He was one of the most well-connected politicians in Kern County history.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department asks court to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Justice Department asked a court Friday to dismiss corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams, with a top official from Washington intervening after federal prosecutors in Manhattan rebuffed his demands to drop the case and some quit in protest.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Ellison tells Minnesota schools to follow law if immigration authorities show up
item.description: Responding to the Trump administration’s decision to allow immigration enforcement in schools, Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison on Friday advised district leaders to ensure that they and any federal officials who arrive at schools follow legal protocols.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Shockman, MPR News

item.title: Trump rolls back CFPB, USAID and Department of Education: Where things stand
item.description: President Trump supercharged his overhaul of government agencies this week that included a fresh round of a widespread firings.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official considering running for California governor if Kamala Harris enters race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a Trump administration official, said Friday that he would consider entering the 2026 California governor race if former Vice President and Democratic Presidential candidate Kamala Harris joins the growing list of candidates.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: The art of the deal? Zelenskyy says a Ukraine-Russia agreement must come through Trump negotiations
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appealed to President Donald Trump's history as a dealmaker on Friday, offering flattering words as he sought the U.S. president's leadership in negotiations that could end the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, MONIKA SCISLOWSKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury, Congress watchdogs launch audits into DOGE access to payment system
item.description: Two government watchdogs have launched audits into the new Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) access to a sensitive federal payment system located at the Treasury Department.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minneapolis City Council postpones proposal to move violence prevention funds to county
item.description: After a lengthy and sometimes heated discussion, the Minneapolis City Council voted Thursday to indefinitely postpone a proposal to have Hennepin County temporarily oversee two of the city’s violence intervention programs.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: DOJ sues NY Gov. Hochul, AG James over immigration policies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, went after New York leaders Wednesday over the state's immigration policies, announcing a lawsuit in the latest effort by the Republican administration to carry out the president's hardline immigration campaign p
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Skyrocketing fees: St. Pete condo owners say they never asked for bailout, only relief
item.description: Condo owners are waiting for answers on whether lawmakers will do anything to ease concerns over rising fees.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Black educators warn of what is lost if Education Department is shuttered
item.description: Black educators are sounding the alarm as President Donald Trump ramps up talk of eliminating the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: GOP senators 'terrified' of crossing Trump, facing Musk-funded challengers
item.description: GOP senators are "terrified" over the prospect of facing primary challengers funded by Elon Musk if they stick their necks out by opposing President Donald Trump's agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
